titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/227

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=182 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:15:52.586344+00 | translation moa=183 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:15:54.467529+00

div.p:
Ric’ dei gratia ecclesie beati Andree humilis minister . vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis  salutem. Sciant tam posteri
quam presentes nos concessisse  presentis scripti munimine confirmasse deo  canonicis apud Edenburg
deo seruientibus  seruituris ecclesiam de kynal cum omnibus eidem iuste pertinentibus tenendam in liberam
 perpetuam elmosinam . Quare uolumus  auctoritate episcopali mandamus ut predicti canonici prenominatam
ecclesiam adeo libere teneant . quiete  honorifice possideant  sicut liberius . quietius .  honorificentius aliquam
ecclesiam in tota diocesi nostra tenent  possident . saluis episcopalibus . hiis testibus . A. archidiacono Laodonie .
Waltero archidiacono de sancto andrea . I. priore de hadintune . Rogero priore de Insula . Roberto clerico de pert .
Alexandro  Willelmo capellanis episcopi . Galfr’ dapifero . magistro Abraham . Odone dapifero . hugone pincerna .

p.cl: Richard by the grace of God humble servant of the church of the blessed Andrew, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Let those to come as well as those present know that we have granted and by the defence of this document made firm to God and to the canons serving God at Edinburgh now and in the future, the church of Kinneil with all things rightfully belonging to it, to be held in free and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will and command by episcopal authority that the aforesaid canons should hold and possess that church as freely, quietly and honourably as they hold and possess any church in the whole of our diocese. Reserving the episcopal dues.

With these witnesses, Andrew archdeacon of Lothian, Walter, archdeacon of St Andrews, Ilbert prior of Haddington, Roger prior of Loch Leven, Robert clerk of Perth, Alexander and William the bishop’s chaplains, Geoffrey the steward, Master Abraham, Odo the steward, Hugh the butler.
